changes.value:
## What

Virtual tags should be visually distinguishable from regular tags. Use a dashed border instead of solid, and slightly different opacity to signal \"computed, not editable\".

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — in `MentionPillInner`, check if the resolved entity has `virtual: true` in its fields, and if so:
  - Use `border-style: dashed` instead of solid
  - Optionally add a subtle icon or prefix (e.g. a small ⚡ or just rely on the dashed border)
  - Add a CSS class like `mention-pill-virtual` for test targeting

The entity store already has the full entity data including the `virtual` field (once VT-1 is done). The `MentionPill` component already resolves the entity, so it can read `virtual` from `entity.fields`.

Need to pass the `virtual` flag down from `MentionPill` to `MentionPillInner` as a prop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Virtual tag pills render with dashed border
- [ ] Regular tag pills still render with solid border (no regression)
- [ ] Visual distinction is clear but not jarring
- [ ] `mention-pill-virtual` CSS class present on virtual tag pills

## Tests
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag entity renders with dashed border / virtual class
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag renders with solid border
- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

#virtual-tags
VT-9: Visual distinction for virtual tag pills

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,25 +1,33 @@
 ## What

-Virtual tags should be visually distinguishable from regular tags. Use a dashed border instead of solid, and slightly different opacity to signal \"computed, not editable\".
+Virtual tags should be visually distinguishable from regular tags. Use a dashed border instead of solid to signal "computed, not editable".
+
+With the three-field architecture, `virtual_tags` is a separate `badge-list` field displayed alongside `tags`. The `BadgeListDisplay` component renders both fields independently — each produces `MentionPill` components. We need pills from the `virtual_tags` field to render differently.
+
+**Two approaches** (pick simplest):
+
+### Option A: Field-level prop on BadgeListDisplay
+`BadgeListDisplay` already knows which field it's rendering. If the field name is `virtual_tags` (or the field has some marker like `editor: none`), pass a `virtual` prop down to `MentionPill` → `MentionPillInner`.

-**Files to modify:**
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — in `MentionPillInner`, check if the resolved entity has `virtual: true` in its fields, and if so:
-  - Use `border-style: dashed` instead of solid
-  - Optionally add a subtle icon or prefix (e.g. a small ⚡ or just rely on the dashed border)
-  - Add a CSS class like `mention-pill-virtual` for test targeting
+### Option B: Entity-level check
+`MentionPill` resolves the tag entity, reads `virtual: true` from its fields, passes to inner.

-The entity store already has the full entity data including the `virtual` field (once VT-1 is done). The `MentionPill` component already resolves the entity, so it can read `virtual` from `entity.fields`.
+Option B is more robust (works regardless of which field renders the pill) but Option A is simpler for the current use case. Recommend Option B.

-Need to pass the `virtual` flag down from `MentionPill` to `MentionPillInner` as a prop.
+**Files to modify:**
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
+  - In `MentionPill`, after resolving entity, check `entity.fields.virtual === true`
+  - Pass `isVirtual` prop to `MentionPillInner`
+  - In `MentionPillInner`, when `isVirtual`: use `border-style: dashed`, add CSS class `mention-pill-virtual`

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Virtual tag pills render with dashed border
 - [ ] Regular tag pills still render with solid border (no regression)
 - [ ] Visual distinction is clear but not jarring
-- [ ] `mention-pill-virtual` CSS class present on virtual tag pills
+- [ ] `mention-pill-virtual` CSS class present on virtual tag pills for test targeting

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag entity renders with dashed border / virtual class
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that tag entity with `virtual: true` renders with dashed border / virtual class
 - [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag renders with solid border
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,34 +1,27 @@
 ## What

-Virtual tags should be visually distinguishable from regular tags. Use a dashed border instead of solid to signal "computed, not editable".
-
-With the three-field architecture, `virtual_tags` is a separate `badge-list` field displayed alongside `tags`. The `BadgeListDisplay` component renders both fields independently — each produces `MentionPill` components. We need pills from the `virtual_tags` field to render differently.
-
-**Two approaches** (pick simplest):
-
-### Option A: Field-level prop on BadgeListDisplay
-`BadgeListDisplay` already knows which field it's rendering. If the field name is `virtual_tags` (or the field has some marker like `editor: none`), pass a `virtual` prop down to `MentionPill` → `MentionPillInner`.
-
-### Option B: Entity-level check
-`MentionPill` resolves the tag entity, reads `virtual: true` from its fields, passes to inner.
+Virtual tag pills should be visually distinguishable from regular tags — dashed border signals "computed, not editable".

-Option B is more robust (works regardless of which field renders the pill) but Option A is simpler for the current use case. Recommend Option B.
+With Option B, virtual tags are NOT in the entity store. `MentionPill` resolves them via the virtual tag metadata fallback (from VT-3). The resolution path itself tells us whether a tag is virtual: if it was resolved from the metadata map rather than the entity store, it's virtual.

 **Files to modify:**
 - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
-  - In `MentionPill`, after resolving entity, check `entity.fields.virtual === true`
-  - Pass `isVirtual` prop to `MentionPillInner`
-  - In `MentionPillInner`, when `isVirtual`: use `border-style: dashed`, add CSS class `mention-pill-virtual`
+  - `MentionPill` already attempts entity store resolution. After VT-3, it falls back to virtual tag metadata.
+  - Track which resolution path succeeded. If virtual metadata: set `isVirtual = true`.
+  - Pass `isVirtual` prop to `MentionPillInner`.
+  - In `MentionPillInner`, when `isVirtual`: use `border-style: dashed`, add CSS class `mention-pill-virtual`.
+
+This is clean because the virtual/non-virtual distinction emerges from the resolution path — no flag on the entity, no extra field check.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Virtual tag pills render with dashed border
 - [ ] Regular tag pills still render with solid border (no regression)
-- [ ] Visual distinction is clear but not jarring
 - [ ] `mention-pill-virtual` CSS class present on virtual tag pills for test targeting
+- [ ] Resolution fallback from VT-3 drives the distinction (no hardcoded slug list)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that tag entity with `virtual: true` renders with dashed border / virtual class
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag renders with solid border
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that tag resolved from virtual metadata renders with dashed border / virtual class
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that entity-store tag renders with solid border
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

 ## Workflow

 ## What

-Virtual tag pills should be visually distinguishable from regular tags — dashed border signals "computed, not editable".
+Virtual tags should be visually distinguishable from regular tags. Use a dashed border instead of solid to signal "computed, not editable".
+
+With the three-field architecture, `virtual_tags` is a separate `badge-list` field displayed alongside `tags`. The `BadgeListDisplay` component renders both fields independently — each produces `MentionPill` components. We need pills from the `virtual_tags` field to render differently.

-With Option B, virtual tags are NOT in the entity store. `MentionPill` resolves them via the virtual tag metadata fallback (from VT-3). The resolution path itself tells us whether a tag is virtual: if it was resolved from the metadata map rather than the entity store, it's virtual.
+**Two approaches** (pick simplest):

+### Option A: Field-level prop on BadgeListDisplay
+`BadgeListDisplay` already knows which field it's rendering. If the field name is `virtual_tags` (or the field has some marker like `editor: none`), pass a `virtual` prop down to `MentionPill` → `MentionPillInner`.
+
+### Option B: Entity-level check
+`MentionPill` resolves the tag entity, reads `virtual: true` from its fields, passes to inner.
+
+Option B is more robust (works regardless of which field renders the pill) but Option A is simpler for the current use case. Recommend Option B.
+

 - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:

-  - In `MentionPillInner`, when `isVirtual`: use `border-style: dashed`, add CSS class `mention-pill-virtual`.
-
-This is clean because the virtual/non-virtual distinction emerges from the resolution path — no flag on the entity, no extra field check.
+  - In `MentionPill`, after resolving entity, check `entity.fields.virtual === true`
+  - Pass `isVirtual` prop to `MentionPillInner`
+  - In `MentionPillInner`, when `isVirtual`: use `border-style: dashed`, add CSS class `mention-pill-virtual`

 - [ ] Regular tag pills still render with solid border (no regression)
+- [ ] Visual distinction is clear but not jarring
 - [ ] `mention-pill-virtual` CSS class present on virtual tag pills for test targeting
-- [ ] Resolution fallback from VT-3 drives the distinction (no hardcoded slug list)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that tag resolved from virtual metadata renders with dashed border / virtual class
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that entity-store tag renders with solid border
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that tag entity with `virtual: true` renders with dashed border / virtual class
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag renders with solid border
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

changes.old_value:
## What

Virtual tag pills should be visually distinguishable from regular tags — dashed border signals "computed, not editable".

With Option B, virtual tags are NOT in the entity store. `MentionPill` resolves them via the virtual tag metadata fallback (from VT-3). The resolution path itself tells us whether a tag is virtual: if it was resolved from the metadata map rather than the entity store, it's virtual.

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
  - `MentionPill` already attempts entity store resolution. After VT-3, it falls back to virtual tag metadata.
  - Track which resolution path succeeded. If virtual metadata: set `isVirtual = true`.
  - Pass `isVirtual` prop to `MentionPillInner`.
  - In `MentionPillInner`, when `isVirtual`: use `border-style: dashed`, add CSS class `mention-pill-virtual`.

This is clean because the virtual/non-virtual distinction emerges from the resolution path — no flag on the entity, no extra field check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Virtual tag pills render with dashed border
- [ ] Regular tag pills still render with solid border (no regression)
- [ ] `mention-pill-virtual` CSS class present on virtual tag pills for test targeting
- [ ] Resolution fallback from VT-3 drives the distinction (no hardcoded slug list)

## Tests
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that tag resolved from virtual metadata renders with dashed border / virtual class
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that entity-store tag renders with solid border
- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
